mission: Compare OLD rendering (with +80px/×1.23 legacy correction) to NEW rendering (1:1 coordinates) to determine which matches the preview image layout described as 'zentriert untereinander' (centered, stacked vertically)
critical_question: Could the OLD (broken) rendering actually match the preview better than the NEW (fixed) rendering?

new_rendering_1to1.layout_description: Image 2 (small logo) positioned ABOVE Image 1 (large image). Image 2 starts at Y=128.3, Image 1 starts at Y=154.6. They overlap vertically - Image 2 is fully within the vertical span of Image 1.
new_rendering_1to1.visual_interpretation: Image 2 appears in the UPPER portion of the design, Image 1 appears slightly below but much larger, creating an overlapping composition

old_rendering_with_legacy_correction.layout_description: Image 2 (small logo) positioned ABOVE Image 1 (large image). Image 2 starts at Y=208.3, Image 1 starts at Y=234.6. They overlap vertically - Image 2 is fully within the vertical span of Image 1. SAME relative positioning as NEW, just shifted down 80px and scaled up 1.23×.
old_rendering_with_legacy_correction.gap_between_elements: -147.6px (negative = overlap, MORE overlap due to 1.23× scaling)
old_rendering_with_legacy_correction.visual_interpretation: Image 2 appears in the UPPER portion of the design, Image 1 appears slightly below but much larger, creating an overlapping composition. ENTIRE layout shifted down 80px.

stacking_analysis.interpretation: NOT 'untereinander' (one below the other). Elements OVERLAP with Image 2 appearing in the upper region and Image 1 occupying a larger area that includes Image 2's vertical position. This is a LAYERED composition, not a vertically stacked one.
stacking_analysis.untereinander_definition: 'Untereinander' typically means elements positioned sequentially with vertical spacing, like a list. This layout has OVERLAPPING elements instead.

new_rendering.alignment: Nearly centered horizontally - centers are 5.6px apart
new_rendering.interpretation: Image 2 is ALMOST horizontally centered relative to Image 1, with Image 2 slightly to the left

old_rendering.interpretation: Image 2 is OFFSET to the left relative to Image 1 center due to incorrect 1.23× scaling

horizontal_positioning_analysis.which_is_more_centered: NEW rendering has better horizontal centering (5.6px offset vs 24.3px offset)

fix_commits.title: FIX: Prevent Double Legacy Correction on Converted Golden Standard Data
FIX: View Wrapper Metadata Extraction in applyLegacyDataCorrection()

timeline_reconstruction.conclusion: Fix was implemented TODAY (Oct 2, 2025). Preview was likely generated BEFORE fix, when legacy correction was incorrectly applied.

paradox_resolution.the_paradox: User says preview shows 'zentriert untereinander' but current rendering doesn't match

possible_explanations.implication: Preview needs to be regenerated with NEW coordinates

new_rendering.description: Small logo (Image 2) appears in upper region, large image (Image 1) positioned slightly below with significant overlap. Nearly centered horizontally (5.6px offset).

old_rendering.description: Small logo (Image 2) appears in upper region, large image (Image 1) positioned slightly below with MORE overlap. NOT centered horizontally (24.3px offset). ENTIRE layout shifted down 80px. Elements are 23% larger.

key_differences: Vertical offset: NEW is 80px higher than OLD
Size: NEW is 18.7% smaller than OLD (1.23× reduction)
Horizontal centering: NEW is better centered (5.6px vs 24.3px offset)
Overlap: OLD has more overlap due to larger element sizes

recommended_action: REGENERATE preview_189542-98.png with NEW (fixed) rendering to verify if it now matches user's description. If preview was generated with OLD coordinates, it needs to be updated to reflect the corrected 1:1 coordinate preservation.

critical_insights.insight_1: OLD and NEW renderings have SAME relative positioning (Image 2 above Image 1 by 26.2px), just different absolute positions and sizes
critical_insights.insight_2: User description 'zentriert untereinander' is ambiguous - could mean horizontally centered (which NEW achieves better) or vertically stacked (which NEITHER achieves - elements overlap)
critical_insights.insight_3: NEW rendering has better horizontal centering (5.6px offset) than OLD (24.3px offset), suggesting NEW is more likely to appear 'zentriert' (centered)
critical_insights.insight_4: Fix was implemented TODAY - timeline suggests preview may predate fix and show OLD (incorrect) rendering
critical_insights.insight_5: Cannot definitively determine which rendering matches preview without viewing actual preview file

confidence_level: 75% - High confidence in coordinate analysis, but cannot verify against actual preview image without file access
final_assessment: The NEW rendering (1:1 coordinates) is mathematically correct and shows better horizontal centering. However, if the preview was generated BEFORE the fix (likely, given timeline), it would show the OLD rendering with +80px offset and ×1.23 scaling. This would explain why user sees a discrepancy. Recommended action: Regenerate preview with current (fixed) rendering to eliminate the discrepancy.
answer_to_critical_question: NO - The OLD (broken) rendering does NOT match the user's description better. The NEW rendering achieves better horizontal centering (5.6px offset vs 24.3px), which aligns with 'zentriert' (centered). The discrepancy likely exists because the preview image was generated with the OLD (incorrect) coordinates before the fix was implemented today.

metadata.methodology: Comparative analysis of OLD vs NEW rendering coordinates, gap calculations, horizontal centering analysis, timeline reconstruction, hypothesis evaluation
